title: viola regina
intro: Viola regina, also known as the wild pansy or heart's ease, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and delicate purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including treating anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Viola regina include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Viola regina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
